question: What is the definition of the abbreviation SG?

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale’s Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature measurement devices, flow meters, level indicators, and analysis instruments, ensuring standardized interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuScale NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design using an electrically heated 5x5 simulated fuel bundle with AREVA HTP™/HMP™ spacer grid technology. The tests, performed at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility, aimed to gather CHF data, validate and develop CHF correlations, determine mixing coefficients, and measure pressure drop characteristics under various hydraulic conditions to support the NuScale Power Module (NPM) application.

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The electrically-heated steam generator test at the SIET facility focused on secondary-side performance using three instrumented tubes with controlled boundary conditions and distinct heating zones to simulate subcooled, boiling, and superheat regions. The testing provided valuable data on pressure fluctuations, flow stability, and steam outlet conditions, supporting the design, safety analysis, and code benchmarking for the NuScale steam generator, and was inspected by the NRC according to relevant procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It describes the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system monitoring and control, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that tables and figures are numbered according to their section, pages are sequentially numbered, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and numbering of text, tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), following specific regulatory guidance. It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure and is clearly marked, with a separate public version provided that excludes such material.
